title: Advanced Arguments: ... (ellipsis)
description: Use the dot-dot-dot argument to pass variable numbers of arguments.

slides.title: The ... Argument
slides.content:
The special argument `...` (ellipsis) allows a function to accept any number of additional arguments. These are often passed on to other functions.

For example, many plotting functions use `...` to pass graphical parameters.

slides.title: Using ...
slides.content: Define a function that collects extra arguments:

slides.title: Capturing ...
slides.content:
You can capture `...` into a list using `list(...)`. This is useful for processing arbitrary arguments.

```r
gather <- function(...) {
  list(...)
}
gather(a = 1, b = 2, "hello")
```

slides.title: Capture Example

quizData.question: What is the purpose of `...` in a function definition?

options.text: To indicate that the function has no arguments
To accept an arbitrary number of additional arguments

quizData.explanation: `...` captures any extra arguments passed to the function.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Use `...` when writing wrapper functions that need to pass arguments to another function without knowing them all in advance.
